title: TextInput Deep Dive
description: Explore all TextInput props: keyboard types, secure entry, multiline, and more.

slides.title: TextInput Review 📝
slides.content:
# The Essential Input Component

`<TextInput>` is the core component for text entry. We've used basic props like `value`, `onChangeText`, and `placeholder`. Now let's explore its full power.

**Controlled vs Uncontrolled** – Always use controlled components (set `value` and `onChangeText`) to keep React state as the single source of truth.

slides.title: Keyboard Configuration ⌨️
slides.content:
## Keyboard Types

Control which keyboard appears:

- `keyboardType="default"` (default)
- `keyboardType="numeric"` – numbers only
- `keyboardType="email-address"` – includes @ and .
- `keyboardType="phone-pad"` – phone dialer
- `keyboardType="url"` – includes .com etc.

## Return Key

`returnKeyType` controls the label on the return key: `done`, `go`, `next`, `search`, `send`, etc.

```jsx
<TextInput
  keyboardType="email-address"
  returnKeyType="send"
/>
```

slides.title: Secure and Multiline
slides.content:
## Secure Text Entry

Use `secureTextEntry={true}` for passwords.

## Multiline

Set `multiline={true}` to allow multiple lines. You can also control the number of lines with `numberOfLines` (affects initial height).

```jsx
<TextInput
  multiline
  numberOfLines={4}
  style={{ height: 100, textAlignVertical: 'top' }}
/>
```

Note: On Android, text in multiline inputs may not align to top by default; use `textAlignVertical: 'top'`.

quizData.question: Which prop would you use to show a numeric keypad?

slides.title: Other Useful Props
slides.content:
## Auto‑Capitalisation and Correction

- `autoCapitalize`: `'none'`, `'sentences'`, `'words'`, `'characters'`.
- `autoCorrect`: `true` (default) or `false`.

## Limits

- `maxLength`: maximum number of characters.
- `editable`: `false` to make read‑only.

## Events

- `onFocus` / `onBlur`
- `onSubmitEditing`: when the submit button is pressed (e.g., return key).
- `onChangeText` (receives new text)
- `onKeyPress` (receives key press event)

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: Use `ref` and `focus()` to programmatically focus an input. Great for moving to next field after submit.
